what: M10 turn-discipline baseline: measured cost structure of real agent sessions before enforcement, the targets the tooling enforces, and how both were validated. Re-measure with: npm run flowmap:turns -- summary

measuredFrom.dedupeRule: assistant lines grouped by message.id (transcripts re-emit the same id once per content block); usage = last seen, tool_use blocks accumulated — tools/flowmap/lib/transcript.mjs is the single parser

definitions.batchRatio: tool_use blocks per tool-bearing assistant API call; 1.0 = every read/write pays a full context re-send for one call's worth of work
definitions.cacheReadTokens: sum of usage.cache_read_input_tokens per session — the re-sent context bill
definitions.tokensToFirstSrcEdit: cumulative (cache_read + cache_creation + input) tokens up to and including the first Edit/Write of a /src/ path — the onboarding-cost proxy
definitions.subagentTokens: sum of subagent_tokens markers in Agent tool results — work delegated off the main thread

aggregate.note: cache reads are ~99% of all tokens processed; session cost ~= apiCalls x resident context. subagentTokens median 0 = execution has been running on the main thread.

baseline.independentCrossCheck: a second parser written before this tooling existed (different code, session set of 17) measured 1.26 overall tool calls per API call and 153.2M total cache reads — concordant with this tool's 1.28 median

batchRatio.rationale: independent reads/writes issued in one response halve-or-better the number of full-context re-sends

tokensToFirstSrcEdit.rationale: the scoped onboarding track (onboard --continue, quiz --scope, docs/flowmap/onboard-cost-design.md) was designed for a 20-30k re-proof; 50k allows slack. Baseline is 63x over target — this metric is expected to look bad until the continue track and subagent delegation are routine

cacheReadTokens.rationale: no pass/fail — it is the product of the other two plus session length; watch the median move

enforcement.measure: tools/flowmap/turns.mjs (npm run flowmap:turns -- summary|check); summary also appended to npm run flowmap:metrics
enforcement.force: tools/flowmap/turn-gate.mjs — PreToolUse hook on Read|Grep|Glob; denies the 4th consecutive single-read turn with an actionable reason, one free retry via marker file, deny/allow-after-deny events land in the session log and surface in the flowmap:metrics gate table under 'turns'

validation.tests: 30/30 across turns.test.mjs, turn-gate.test.mjs, metrics.test.mjs (node --test), hermetic via FLOWMAP_ROOT
validation.toolingMap: flowmap:tooling:verify green with the new modules mapped (43/43 src pointers resolve); flowmap:roadmap:audit green
validation.liveFire: synthetic 4-single-read-streak transcript: deny (exit 2, reason names the streak) -> marker written -> identical retry allowed (exit 0) -> marker consumed -> deny + allow-after-deny events recorded, isolated under FLOWMAP_ROOT
validation.hookTiming: originally verified 2026-07-04 (pre-#46 harness): in-flight assistant message present at PreToolUse time. FALSIFIED 2026-07-04 session-5 live-fire: current harness runs the hook BEFORE the message is persisted — deny observed on the 5th lone read naming a streak of 4; gate now documents THRESHOLD+1 effective behavior and the retry check uses <= to be correct under both regimes.
validation.performance: turn-gate parses a real 4.3MB transcript in ~30ms wall — negligible per-read overhead
validation.realData: summary over 18 real sessions matches the pre-tooling measurement (see independentCrossCheck)

reassessment.how: after ~1 week of sessions with the gate live and subagent delegation in use, run: npm run flowmap:turns -- summary, and compare medians to baseline above
reassessment.workingMeans: batchRatio median >=2.0, main-thread cacheReadTokens median at or below half of 7.0M, tokensToFirstSrcEdit median <50000 on continue-track sessions, subagentTokens median >0
reassessment.ifResultsArePoorer: keep the numbers — update this file with a dated 'observed' block per re-measurement rather than deleting the baseline; a failed enforcement is a finding, not an embarrassment (precedent: the onboarding+quiz rework that raised costs)
